Name: axi_sram
Description: Lightweight verification plan for the AXI4 SRAM (data + CHERI tag) block

Name: Main_spec
Description: Covers the "SRAM specification" section of doc/ref/arch.md (lines 43-76). Context-only lines with no testable behaviour: 21k32t (firmware stack/heap usage) and a3l94p (SRAM unused once DRAM active).

Name: interface_geometry
Description: The SRAM block has four ports: clock / reset / AXI4 request in / AXI4 response out

Name: sram_geometry
Description:
Confirm DUT parameterisation matches spec:
* AxiDataWidth = 64 (8-byte word)
* CapSize = 128-bit (16-byte capability)
* 1 CHERI tag bit per 128-bit aligned region
* SramMemSize = 128 KiB

Name: Data_Path
Description: Translation of AXI4 requests into the SRAM interface; data integrity

Name: Clock_and_Reset
Description: Clock and reset input behaviour

Name: rst_sanity
Description: DUT comes out of reset cleanly and accepts the first transaction

Name: init_value_undefined
Description: Initial SRAM and tag contents are undefined at start-up; no read should be expected to return a known value before a write

Name: Basic_access
Description: Single-beat aligned 64-bit read/write correctness

Name: write_read
Description: Single-beat 8-byte write followed by readback returns identical data (also underpins execute-from-SRAM reads)

Name: execute_from_sram
Description: Instruction-flavoured reads (arprot[2]=1) return stored words identically to data reads; a short program is written then fetched as single reads and a sequential burst
Comment: For an SRAM executability == readability; only the arprot=instruction dimension is SRAM-specific

Name: address_boundaries
Description: Write/read first (0x0) and last valid word (0x1FFF8) and last capability slot (0x1FFF0)

Name: data_all_bits
Description: Walk a 1 and a 0 through all 64 data bits to catch stuck-at faults

Name: aligned_only
Description: Only aligned 64-bit accesses are exercised; misaligned data accesses are not expected/supported

Name: Bursts
Description: AXI4 burst handling

Name: burst_last
Description: Last beat of a burst asserts rlast/expects wlast correctly
Comment: rlast placement currently enforced implicitly by the cocotb VIP; TODO add an explicit SV rlast assertion before switching VIPs

Name: resp_id_match
Description: Response (B/R) AXI4 ID equals the originating request ID
Comment: Currently enforced implicitly by the cocotb VIP's ID-based response routing; TODO add an explicit SV ID-match assertion before switching VIPs

Name: bounded_response
Description: Every request receives a response within a bounded time proportional to burst length

Name: Concurrency
Description: Multiple AXI transactions in flight

Name: concurrent_data_tag
Description: A plain-data write and a capability write issued concurrently both complete and read back correctly

Name: CHERI_tags
Description: CHERI tag bit storage and read-back behaviour (1 bit per 128-bit region; communicated via wuser/ruser)

Name: Tag_write
Description: Conditions under which a tag is set to 1

Name: tag_write
Description: Full 128-bit aligned write as a single 2-beat burst with wuser=1 sets the tag

Name: tag_write
Description: Each data field has a corresponding tag field which can be accessed in the same transaction

Name: no_tag_single_beat
Description: wuser=1 on a single-beat (awlen=0) write must NOT set the tag (not a full region in a single burst; is_w_cap_sized requires awlen=1)
Comment: Corner: tag only on full capability burst

Name: no_tag_misaligned
Description: 2-beat wuser=1 burst starting at a non-16-byte-aligned address must NOT set the tag (is_w_cap_aligned requires addr[3:0]==0)

Name: no_tag_two_bursts
Description: Writing both halves of a 128-bit region as two separate single-beat bursts must NOT set the tag; the tag requires a single 2-beat capability burst
Comment: Corner: full region covered across two separate bursts still leaves the tag 0

Name: assert_wuser_not_full_cap
Description: Assertion fires when wuser=1 on a write that is not part of a full capability write

Name: assert_wuser_mismatch
Description: Assertion fires on wuser mismatch (one half of the capability valid the other invalid in the same transaction)

Name: Tag_clear
Description: Conditions under which a previously-set tag is cleared

Name: tag_cleared_by_write
Description: A plain data write to either word of a tagged capability slot clears the tag for the whole region
Comment: Corner: any non-cap write zeroes the tag bit

Name: partial_strobe_clears_tag
Description: A sub-64-bit (partial write-strobe) write to a tagged slot clears the tag

Name: subword_read_clears_tag
Description: Reads of only part of a 64-bit value from a valid cap region return data but with the tag cleared

Name: Tag_read
Description: Per-beat ruser (CHERI tag) generation on reads

Name: cap_both_ruser_set
Description: Both ruser flits of a valid capability read are 1 (core ANDs the two together to determine validity)
Comment: Checks both flits=1 for a valid cap and both flits=0 for an untagged one. af8sx6 asymmetric (one flit 0) is N/A here: one tag bit per region drives both flits equal; cross-region asymmetry is tag-controller scope

Name: burst_read_mixed_tags
Description: A read spanning a tagged and an untagged capability slot returns the correct per-beat ruser for each
Comment: Per-beat tags across multi-beat (awlen>1) bursts are the tag controller's responsibility, not axi_sram (which gates ruser on awlen==1); out of scope for this block

Name: Tag_isolation
Description: Tags are stored independently and in separate memory from data

Name: tag_isolation
Description: Writing/clearing the tag of one 128-bit slot must not affect an adjacent slot's tag

Name: tag_separate_memory
Description: Tags are stored in a separate memory block from data (enables future bulk tag reads)
Comment: Structural property verified by inspection (distinct u_tag_ram and u_ram prim_ram_1p instances); not observable at the AXI boundary, so not a runtime assertion

Name: Error_handling
Description: Out-of-range and error-response behaviour

Name: out_of_range_error
Description: Accesses outside the SRAM range return an error response including when only part of a burst is out of range
Comment: Enforced by the SoC interconnect / address decode upstream, not axi_sram; out of scope for this block

Name: atomics_excluded
Description: Atomic (ATOP) accesses are excluded and dont need to be supported

Name: Stress_random
Description: Randomised back-to-back access sweeps
Comment: randomization should be seeded for reproducibility

Name: random_data
Description: N random (aligned addr, data) write/read pairs across the address space

Name: random_capabilities
Description: N random 16-byte-aligned capability write/read pairs verifying data and tag

Name: Coverage
Description: General coverage collection (functional + code) for the AXI SRAM

Name: coverage_note
Description: NOTE: Coverage is intentionally NOT defined in this lightweight plan. Functional covergroups (AXI burst length/size, address regions, tag set/clear, ruser combinations) and code coverage (block/toggle/expression/FSM) should be added once the verification approach matures. The metric model and coverage goals will be decided at a later stage.
